cards.title: Hydrate
cards.short: Sip water now. Don't wait until you're thirsty.
cards.long: Sip water steadily in the time before the match. Small amounts often. Avoid large gulps right before you walk on court.
cards.heat_suffix: It's warm out today — staying on top of fluids matters even before you start playing.

cards.title: Snack timing
cards.short: Pre-match snack 60–90 min before match time.
cards.long: If you haven't eaten yet, now's the window. Something easy — a banana, crackers, a small sandwich. Light enough to sit well on court.

cards.title: Light movement
cards.short: 5–10 min of light movement to wake the body up.
cards.long: A short walk, easy jogging, whatever your kid usually does before a match. You know what works for them — now's the time. Nothing intense.

cards.title: Pack check
cards.long: Quick bag check before heading to the court. Rackets, towel, water bottle, snack for after, sunscreen. Anything missing? Now's the time to grab it.

cards.title: Mental prep
cards.short: Take 3 deep breaths. One match at a time.
cards.long: One match at a time. What's done in practice is done. Take 3 slow deep breaths, shake out the hands, and walk on court ready to compete.

cards.title: Cool down
cards.short: Walk for a few minutes off-court. Don't sit right away.
cards.long: A short walk off-court helps the body start recovering. Don't plop down immediately — a few minutes of easy walking before you rest makes a difference.
cards.heat_suffix: Find shade as soon as you can — get out of direct sun.

cards.title: Refuel
cards.short: Check your food card — eat if the gap allows.
cards.long: If you have time, eating something real between matches is worth it. Check your food card for nearby options. Something with carbs and a little protein. Keep it light.

cards.title: Rest in shade
cards.short: Rest in shade. Feet up if possible.
cards.long: Find a shaded spot and get off your feet. Elevate your legs if you can — lean them against a bag, a chair, whatever's around. Even 10 minutes makes a difference.
cards.heat_suffix: Today's conditions make shade especially important — don't skip this one.

cards.title: Gear reset
cards.short: Dry shirt, dry socks, reapply sunscreen.
cards.long: If you have a spare shirt or socks, now's the time to swap. Reapply sunscreen. A quick change out of wet gear helps more than it sounds.

cards.title: Mental reset
cards.short: Last match is done. Reset for the next one.
cards.long: Whatever happened in that match — good or bad — it's over. Take a breath, let it go, and start thinking about the next match fresh. One match at a time.

cards.title: Keep sipping
cards.long: Keep sipping water through the evening — not all at once, steadily. If you brought electrolyte mix and it's been a hot day, this is a reasonable time to use it if tolerated.
cards.heat_suffix: Today was warm — fluids through the evening matter more than usual.

cards.title: Easy movement & shower
cards.short: Easy movement or stretching if your kid likes it, then shower.
cards.long: Easy movement or stretching if your kid likes it — nothing intense. A shower helps the body feel like the day is done. Nothing structured required.

cards.title: Real meal
cards.short: A balanced meal within 1–2 hours of finishing.
cards.long: Aim for a solid, balanced meal within 1–2 hours of finishing the day — something with protein, carbs, and vegetables if possible. Don't skip dinner because you're tired.

cards.title: Prep for tomorrow
cards.long: Pack the bag tonight — rackets, towel, water bottle, snacks. Lay out the outfit. One less thing to think about in the morning when you're rushing out the door.

cards.title: Sleep
cards.long: Earlier is better, especially if tomorrow has an early first match. Turn off screens a bit before bed if you can. Sleep is when recovery actually happens.
